name: Blueberry Oat Bars
recipeIngredient: 1 3/4 cups Quaker Oats, uncooked (quick or old-fashioned)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Grease a 11x7-inch glass baking dish.
3. In a large bowl, combine oats, flour, brown sugar, nuts, baking soda, and salt.
4. Add margarine and mix until crumbly.
5. Reserve 3/4 cup of the mixture. Press the remaining mixture onto the bottom of the prepared dish.
6. Bake for 10 minutes.
7. In a separate bowl, combine blueberries, granulated sugar, and 2 tablespoons water. Bring to a boil, then simmer uncovered for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
8. In another small bowl, mix the remaining tablespoon water, cornstarch, and lemon juice until well combined.
9. Gradually stir the cornstarch mixture into the blueberry mixture and cook for about 30 seconds or until thickened.
10. Spread the blueberry mixture over the partially baked base, leaving at least a quarter-inch from the edge.
11. Sprinkle the reserved oat mixture on top.
12. Bake for an additional 18 to 20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown.
13. Remove from the oven and let cool on a wire rack before cutting into bars.
14. Store in a tightly covered container.
